source_test: HEC.FDA.ModelTest/unittests/DefaultThresholdShould.cs (ProduceSameThresholdStageRegardlessOfDamageCategoryCount, BeCreatedAutomaticallyWhenNoThresholdProvided, NotOverrideUserProvidedDefaultThreshold) -- exercises ImpactAreaScenarioSimulation.cs SetupPerformanceThresholds (136-160) + ComputeDefaultThreshold (707-722) + ComputeTotalStageDamage (695-706) via a single DETERMINISTIC pass, not the full Monte Carlo compute loop (Phase 5 Task 10's job).
note: construct = {impact_area_id, flow_frequency: {type,params} (Uniform(0,100000,1000)), flow_stage: {xs:[0,100000], ys:[{Uniform,[0,0,10]},{Uniform,[0,30,10]}]} (MakeFlowStage), stage_damage: [{damage_category, asset_category, xs:[0,15,30], ys:[{Uniform,[0,0,10]},{Uniform,[0,600000,10]},{Uniform,[0,600000,10]}]}, ...] (one entry per MakeStageDamage call -- the two_categories case adds a second entry with a different damage_category so it's a genuinely distinct AggregatedConsequencesBinned/ConsequenceFrequencyFunctions bucket, matching the real DefaultThresholdShould test), additional_threshold: {threshold_id, type, value} OPTIONAL (NotOverrideUserProvidedDefaultThreshold's pre-registered ID-0 Threshold via WithAdditionalThreshold, built with ConvergenceCriteria(1,1) matching the C# test)}. method 'default_threshold_value', args [min_iterations, max_iterations] (always [1,1], matching every DefaultThresholdShould test's ConvergenceCriteria(minIterations:1, maxIterations:1)): calls setup_performance_thresholds(ConvergenceCriteria(min,max)) directly (this port's public-access relaxation of the private C# SetupPerformanceThresholds, matching Task 7/8's identical rationale for can_compute/initialize_consequence_histograms/populate_random_numbers/get_frequency_stage_sample -- SetupPerformanceThresholds alone reproduces DefaultThresholdShould's oracles without needing Task 10's not-yet-implemented Monte Carlo ComputeIterations loop, since it runs its own internal deterministic iteration-1 pass and Threshold.ThresholdValue is never touched by ComputeIterations afterward), then reads results().performance_by_thresholds().get_threshold(0).threshold_value(). All values PIN (gate-captured against the real C#, never hand-derived) except the user-override case, which is a direct pass-through of the literal 20.0 supplied to the Threshold ctor (verified via the gate too, not hand-derived).
